frameworks.name: efficient encoding with Bayesian decoding
frameworks.one_line: efficient encoding plus Bayesian decoding in a slowly changing environment

frameworks.name: ideal Bayesian observer
frameworks.one_line: pure Bayesian observer fails to capture concurrent attractive and repulsive biases

frameworks.name: attractive serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: short-timescale attraction toward recent perceptual history

core_question: Do attractive and repulsive history biases coexist in visual perception, and what computational principles produce them?
key_assumptions: encoding resources are allocated to match recent stimulus statistics
decoding uses Bayesian inference over recent history
the environment changes slowly relative to trial timescale
manipulations: timescale of past stimulus influence (short-term vs. minutes-long)
separation of attractive vs. repulsive bias contributions
key_findings: perceptual decisions are concurrently attracted to short-term history
perceptual decisions are repelled from stimuli experienced up to minutes earlier
an ideal Bayesian observer alone cannot reproduce this temporal pattern
an efficient-encoding plus Bayesian-decoding observer captures the pattern
interpretations: concurrent attractive and repulsive biases reflect joint constraints of efficiency and stability in visual processing
